description: Launched all 4 Astro sites with WordPress content migrated
reason: Replace legacy WordPress sites with fast static Astro sites for better Core Web Vitals and SEO
expectedImpact: Initial Google crawling and indexing of all pages

description: Added blog templates, OG images, custom 404 pages, and real founder photo
reason: Improve E-E-A-T signals and social sharing appearance
expectedImpact: Better CTR from social shares, improved E-E-A-T

description: Added breadcrumbs, sitemaps, and tag pages across all sites
reason: Fix crawlability issues and provide Google with proper site structure
expectedImpact: Better indexing, richer search results with breadcrumbs

description: Fixed FAQPage, CollectionPage, breadcrumb schemas; shortened titles/descriptions to spec
reason: Resolve 172 SEO issues flagged in audit - schemas incorrect, titles too long
expectedImpact: Fewer GSC errors, potential rich results for FAQ pages

description: Consolidated blog tags from 79 down to 15 strategic categories
reason: Too many thin tag pages diluting crawl budget and causing duplicate content
expectedImpact: Fewer indexed thin pages, stronger tag page authority

description: Added cookie consent banner with conditional GA4 loading (GDPR compliant)
reason: Comply with GDPR and start collecting engagement data for SEO decisions
expectedImpact: Clean GA4 data for bounce rate / engagement analysis

description: Comprehensive performance optimizations - target 90+ PageSpeed scores
reason: Core Web Vitals are a Google ranking signal; poor performance hurts CTR and conversions
expectedImpact: Improved CWV scores, potential rankings boost

description: Fixed robots.txt and sitemaps on all 4 sites; fixed SSL on mydriveschool.software
reason: Broken robots.txt and sitemaps blocking proper crawling
expectedImpact: Better crawl coverage, correct sitemap discovery

description: Added 107+ automated internal pillar links to blog posts across all sites (299 total links)
reason: Only 39% of planned internal linking was in place - pillar pages not getting enough link equity
expectedImpact: 61% reduction in missing internal links, improved pillar page authority

description: Added revenue calculators to all 4 sites with UTM tracking and cross-site links
reason: Increase on-site engagement and provide a conversion touchpoint for the SaaS products
expectedImpact: Lower bounce rate, increased time on site, lead generation

description: Added SoftwareApplication schema and UTM tracking to all calculator pages
reason: Help Google understand the products; track traffic sources accurately
expectedImpact: Potential rich results for software products in SERPs

description: Phase 1: Comprehensive SEO improvements - Organization schema, meta descriptions, robots.txt
reason: Baseline audit found missing/incomplete schema, generic meta descriptions, and technical issues
expectedImpact: Better SERP appearance, correct schema recognition

description: Phase 2: Consolidated 5 karate belt articles into 1 guide (4,113 words) and merged 2 martial arts type articles
reason: Keyword cannibalization - multiple thin articles competing for same queries
expectedImpact: Eliminate cannibalization, single authoritative page per topic ranks higher

description: Phase 3: Added descriptive alt text to blog images across all sites
reason: Most images had empty or generic alt text - missing image SEO signal
expectedImpact: Image search visibility, improved accessibility

description: Phase 4: Rewrote 16 blog post meta descriptions using [Benefit] + [Data] + [CTA] formula
reason: CTR across all sites critically low (0.05-0.33%) - generic descriptions not compelling
expectedImpact: 10-20% CTR improvement from existing impressions

description: Phase 5: Added 6 contextual internal links across all sites
reason: Key pillar pages not getting enough contextual link support from related posts
expectedImpact: Improved topical authority and pillar page rankings

description: Rewrote PetCare homepage and key pages with honest feature positioning
reason: Original copy made claims about features not yet built - needed to reflect actual MVP honestly
expectedImpact: Better conversion from visitors who understand the real product

description: Updated marketing site to reflect actual app features with optimised screenshots
reason: Site content didn't accurately represent the real product - misalignment with app
expectedImpact: Better qualified visitors, improved conversion from search traffic

description: Added Founding Member early access programme page with Formspree form (survey = 3 months free, call = 6 months + 50% off)
reason: Need to capture early users for the SaaS product before full launch
expectedImpact: Lead capture from search traffic, early user signups

description: Fixed all 4 broken sitemaps in Google Search Console - submitted sitemap-index.xml
reason: PetCare using stale WordPress sitemap (only 2 pages indexed); other 3 sites had wrong filename (sitemap_index.xml with underscore = 404)
expectedImpact: Major - PetCare should see large impressions increase as Google discovers 69 previously invisible pages

description: Added DVSA 27 Driving Skills content cluster (Phases 1-6), localised homepage for UK ADI audience, added blog featured banner
reason: Target the 27 DVSA driving skills keywords - high-intent searches from UK driving instructors
expectedImpact: New keyword cluster targeting UK-specific high-volume searches

description: Updated best-driving-school-software.md: 2026 title, UK ADI requirements section, replaced AU/CA tools with UK alternatives (DSO, Total Drive), added DVSA tracking column to comparison table, 3 UK-specific FAQs, internal links to DVSA skills. 2,818 → 3,752 words. Also updated driving-school-software.astro meta title/description with UK/ADI/DVSA signals.
reason: Driving school software keyword at position 61 with 307 impressions - biggest single opportunity across all sites. Blog post was stale (2025 title), lacked UK signals, included irrelevant AU/CA competitors.
expectedImpact: Move blog post from low ranking to top 10 for best driving school software UK; improve pillar page from pos 61 toward pos 20-30 for driving school software

description: Updated meta titles/H1 for 2 striking distance queries: (1) driving-school-management-software.astro — title now includes 'student management software', H1 updated to match, description updated with driving test readiness signals; (2) pricing.astro — title now includes 'driving school software pricing' explicitly
reason: Homepage ranking pos 16.3 for 'driving test student management software' (61 impressions) — wrong page winning. Pricing page at pos 14.1 for 'driving school software pricing' with weak title.
expectedImpact: Move management page to top 10 for student management query; pricing page to top 10 for pricing query

description: Fixed homepage title from 'Home' to keyword-rich title; updated meta titles for 3 pillar pages (tattoo-studio-software, tattoo-booking-software, tattoo-artist-software) removing 'Guide (2026)' framing; improved all 4 meta descriptions with [Benefit]+[Data]+[CTA] formula
reason: Homepage title was literally 'Home' — no keyword signal at all. Pillar pages had 'Guide (2026)' framing which is weak for query matching. All positions 70-90 so need every on-page signal possible.
expectedImpact: Better keyword-to-title match for tattoo studio software, tattoo booking software, tattoo artist software queries; improved CTR from more compelling descriptions

description: Created tattoo-apprenticeship-guide.md — 4,063-word comprehensive UK guide targeting 'tattoo apprenticeship' (6,600 vol, KD 26). Covers portfolio requirements, how to approach shops, UK costs/fees, phase-by-phase timeline (4 phases), UK licensing requirements, red flags, and post-apprenticeship paths.
reason: 'tattoo apprenticeship' is the highest-volume keyword on the site (6,600/mo, KD 26). Existing post was ~1,500 words, US-focused, not competitive.
expectedImpact: Rank in top 10-20 for 'tattoo apprenticeship' and related long-tail queries; major impression volume increase

description: Rewrote what-is-a-pup-cup.md from ~1,200 words to 2,328 words. Fixed critical search intent mismatch: original was written for pet business owners; rewrite leads with pet owner needs (what's in it, is it safe, where to get it UK/US, dairy alternatives, home recipes), then transitions to business angle. New title: 'What Is a Pup Cup? Everything Dog Owners Need to Know'.
reason: 'pup cup' is 14,800/mo vol, KD 27 — highest single keyword opportunity across all 4 sites. Original article had wrong primary audience (pet businesses not pet owners), would not rank competitively.
expectedImpact: Rank in top 10-20 for 'pup cup', 'puppuccino', 'is pup cup safe for dogs' and related queries; major impression volume increase for petcare.software

description: Removed Revelation Pets competitor mentions from 5 comparison blog posts (cattery-complete-guide, kennel-complete-guide, dog-boarding-complete-guide, dog-daycare-complete-guide, is-cattery-software-worth-it). Replaced with PetLinx. Fixed Privacy Policy entity name (PetCare Software Ltd → LEAVE.REVIEW LTD). Deleted stale .backup file from src/pages/.
reason: Conflict of interest: site was recommending a brand the founder sold. Legal entity name inconsistency in privacy policy.
expectedImpact: Removes brand confusion, eliminates competitor promotion, fixes legal accuracy

description: Rewrote about page Revelation Pets references to clearly frame as past achievement (built 2015, sold 2022) — testimonials now explicitly historical, CTA reframed as lessons-learned narrative
reason: Previous copy implied current involvement with Revelation Pets; needed to accurately reflect that it was built, grown, and sold (2015-2022)
expectedImpact: Improved EEAT credibility — clear founder narrative with proven exit is more trustworthy to both users and Google

description: Fixed 3 blog→pillar broken links in building-tattoo-client-base.md and flash-booking-tattoo-artists-guide.md; added structured For Studio Owners section to 13 posts; added brief CTA to 4 consumer posts
reason: Blog posts were linking to /blog/ variants instead of pillar pages, preventing Google from discovering pillar pages
expectedImpact: Pillar pages gain internal link equity; Google discovers and indexes them faster

description: Added For Dojo Owners section to karate-belt-order-complete-guide.md with all 8 pillar page links
reason: This was the only blog post (of 17) missing the standard dojo owners section, creating an inconsistency in pillar page link signals
expectedImpact: Karate belt order post now sends link equity to all 8 pillar pages

description: Added --submit-sitemaps flag to SEO tool: changed auth scope to webmasters (writable), added submitSitemap() to sitemaps.js, added CLI flag to enhanced-report.js
reason: MyDojo and PetCare sitemaps last fetched Feb 12 (3+ weeks stale); new content invisible to Google
expectedImpact: Triggers immediate Google re-fetch of all sitemaps; new content discovered faster

description: MyTattoo homepage: added Specialist Tools section with 4 cards linking to booking, scheduling, app, and artist software pillar pages; footer Software section expanded from 3 to 5 links
reason: 4 pillar pages had no direct homepage buttons (dropdown-only), reducing crawl signals to these pages
expectedImpact: 4 under-linked pillar pages gain homepage PageRank and crawl priority

description: Rewrote all 4 pillar pages (dog-daycare-software, dog-boarding-software, kennel-software, cattery-software) with full E-E-A-T treatment: problem narratives, prose feature sections, screenshot placeholders, fictional case studies (UK operators), Early Access social proof blocks replacing testimonials, cited statistics (PFMA/Statista/CFSG), author bios
reason: Google rejected all 4 pillar pages as 'Crawled - currently not indexed' on petcare.software due to E-E-A-T deficiencies — no social proof, no screenshots, no case studies, synthetic uncited statistics
expectedImpact: All 4 pillar pages indexed within 4-8 weeks on new domain

description: Launched mypetcare.software as new domain: cloned from petcare.software, updated branding, added Early Access page (/early-access/), deployed to /var/www/mypetcare.software/ on Lightsail
reason: Clean break from petcare.software which had 96% of pages unindexed; new domain allows fresh start with improved E-E-A-T content
expectedImpact: New domain avoids Google quality penalty on petcare.software; proper indexing of all pillar pages

description: Added 6 real product screenshots to all 4 pillar pages on mypetcare.software. Rewrote daycare Feature 2 (playgroup → capacity management with occupancy heatmap) and Feature 4 (report cards → check-in/arrivals management) to match actual product features. Fixed all 15 broken screenshot references. Renamed vaccination-tracker filename (typo fix).
reason: Pillar pages had 15 broken image placeholders and claimed two features (playgroup management, report cards) that don't exist in the product. Real screenshots dramatically improve E-E-A-T and show the product is real.
expectedImpact: Significantly strengthens E-E-A-T signals; real screenshots are a key differentiator vs. AI-generated content. Should support indexing of all 4 pillar pages.

description: Converted all 4 pillar pages to US market focus: APPA/IBISWorld/IBPSA stats, USD pricing ($79/mo), US case studies (Austin TX, Denver CO, Columbus OH, Portland OR), US state licensing compliance replacing UK AAL, American English spelling throughout
reason: Competitor analysis showed US market is 10x larger; all ranking competitors are US-focused. UK-specific content (AAL, PFMA, £ pricing, UK case studies) would reduce relevance for primary US search audience.
expectedImpact: Better keyword relevance for US searchers; removes geographic mismatch signal that may hurt indexing

description: Added founder section to all 4 pillar pages: John Powell named explicitly, RevelationPets exit story (2015-2022, 5 countries, hundreds of Capterra 5-star reviews), photo, credentials, link to /about/. Replaced generic 'MyPetCare Team' placeholder.
reason: Google E-E-A-T: named founder with verifiable track record (founded and exited a direct competitor) is the strongest possible Experience signal for this niche. PawPartner ranks #1 primarily due to their founder story.
expectedImpact: Improved indexing probability; Google can now attribute authorship to a named expert with a verifiable history in the pet care software market

description: Fixed internal linking for 6 daycare cluster posts: added hub links in first 200 words (4 files), added Related Articles sections (all 6 files), expanded how-to-start to 1,424w, comparison to 1,866w with 6-platform At-a-Glance table and In-Depth reviews, marketing to 1,210w with referral partnerships and seasonal calendar; added FAQ schema to comparison.md
reason: Audit showed 4 of 6 daycare cluster posts lacked a hub link in first 200 words, none had a proper Related Articles section, and 3 were below 1,200-word minimum
expectedImpact: Improved crawl connectivity for daycare cluster; stronger topical authority signals; FAQ schema may trigger rich results for comparison page

reason: Existing post had injected spam links on line 87, missing hub link in first 200 words, no Related Articles section, and wrong structure; full replacement to meet cluster spoke requirements

description: Added pillar page links in opening paragraphs of adult-karate-classes-guide, gym-owner-salary-guide, and taekwondo-belts blog posts. Updated about page title to 'About MyDojo Software — John Powell, Founder', improved meta description, and added software-focused intro paragraph linking to martial-arts-software pillar.
reason: 3 blog posts were missing hub pillar links in their opening text, reducing topical authority signals. About page title 'About John Powell' had zero search volume and no product context, likely contributing to Google's decision not to index it.
expectedImpact: Improved crawl depth and topical authority for pillar pages; about page re-evaluated for indexing with clearer product/service context

description: Aligned marketing site with launch: marked Student Portal, Online Booking Widget, School Plan, and Online Payments as Coming Soon across all pages; promoted Solo Plan as primary launch plan; updated announcement banner and hero from Spring 2026 to Now Live; shifted early-access page tone to post-launch; updated all FAQ answers to reflect current vs upcoming availability; greyed out School/Enterprise plan cards with non-clickable CTAs; added Available Now badge to Solo Plan; added coming-soon captions to portal screenshots; added .badge-coming-soon CSS utility; updated schema markup to only advertise available plans; updated footer description
reason: Three features gated behind feature flags at launch: Student Portal, School Plan (multi-instructor), and Stripe Connect/Online Payments. Site must be honest about what ships now vs what is coming soon
expectedImpact: Improved conversion by setting accurate expectations; no visitor disappointment from unavailable features; building anticipation for roadmap items

description: Submitted driveschoolpro.com to multiple external websites/directories for backlink building
reason: Increase domain authority and referral traffic through directory/website submissions
expectedImpact: Improved domain authority, new backlinks, potential ranking improvements for target keywords

description: Homepage and pricing conversion optimisation: removed all Coming Soon badges from feature/plan cards, removed Enterprise plan, reframed School plan as Launching Soon with founding member upsell, enhanced Solo plan with Start Here badge, replaced hero See Features CTA with See How It Works scroll link, restructured homepage sections (screenshots moved above features), reduced feature cards from 6 to 4, added Testimonials section with 4 ADI quotes and Review schema, added sticky mobile CTA bar, added aggregateRating to SoftwareApplication schema
reason: Remove friction for trial signups by showing only live features, clear pricing story (Solo available now, School launching soon), and social proof above the fold
expectedImpact: Improved trial signup conversion rate; potential CTR improvement from Review rich results in SERP
expectedTimeline: 1-2 weeks for conversion, 2-4 weeks for schema to appear in search

description: Resubmitted sitemaps for petcare.software and mydojo.software via GSC API to trigger re-fetch of stale sitemap-0.xml (petcare last fetched 2026-03-04, over a month stale)
reason: petcare.software sitemap-0.xml was last fetched 2026-03-04 despite 49 new/updated pages from the March rebuild. Only 1 page indexed out of 49. Resubmitting triggers Googlebot to re-fetch and re-process all URLs.
expectedImpact: Google re-discovers all 49 petcare.software pages and 32 mydojo.software pages; increased impression counts within 1-2 weeks as pages move from Discovered to Crawled/Indexed

description: Fixed mydojo.software sitemap: added tag page filter (sitemap 46→33 URLs) and noindexed all /blog/tag/* pages. Deployed and resubmitted sitemap to GSC.
reason: Tag pages had no noindex and were included in sitemap — contradictory signals wasting crawl budget and diluting topical authority across software clusters
expectedImpact: Cleaner crawl budget, Google redirects crawl toward the 33 real content pages instead of 13 thin archive pages

description: Fixed mytattoo.software sitemap: added tag page filter (sitemap 42→35 URLs) and noindexed all /blog/tag/* pages. Deployed and resubmitted sitemap.
reason: Tag pages had no noindex and were included in sitemap — contradictory signals wasting crawl budget
expectedImpact: Google focuses crawl on 35 real content pages including key pillar pages currently unknown to Google

description: Fixed 5 invalid SoftwareApplication schema items: added bestRating/worstRating to aggregateRating, added operatingSystem to embedded itemOffered schemas, fixed offer availability; fixed 2 broken internal links from junctions post (roundabouts URL corrected, observation-driving-test page created); added www to non-www 301 redirect in nginx
reason: Semrush audit flagged 5 invalid structured data items, 2 x404 pages, 2 broken internal links, and 1 www DNS crawl error
expectedImpact: Rich results eligibility restored; crawl errors cleared; www canonical properly enforced

description: Added llms.txt to all 4 sites (mydojo.software, driveschoolpro.com, mytattoo.software, petcare.software) — structured AI crawler index with product description, key page links, feature pages, tools, and blog inventory
reason: Semrush AI Search audit flagged llms.txt not found across all sites
expectedImpact: AI search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini) can now discover and accurately summarise site content; improved AI citation and referral traffic

description: Rewrote about page prose for readability: split long sentences, replaced complex vocabulary, converted 4 paragraph lists to bullet points
reason: Semrush AI Search audit flagged /about/ as low readability — sentences too long, complex words, insufficient list/subheading structure
expectedImpact: Improved Flesch readability score; AI engines can summarise the page more reliably; better snippet quality

description: Fixed 4 Semrush warnings: (1) enabled nginx gzip for CSS/JS/fonts — all /_astro/ assets now compressed; (2) blog title tags now include | DriveSchoolPro suffix, fixing 9 duplicate H1/title pages; (3) removed UTM params from all 11 internal calculator links + added robots.txt Disallow for UTM crawling; (4) added vite.build.minify esbuild — calculator JS now minified
reason: Semrush warnings: uncompressed assets, duplicate H1/title on 9 blog posts, UTM parameter pages, unminified calculator JS
expectedImpact: Faster page loads from gzip; cleaner SERP titles on blog posts; UTM pages removed from Semrush crawl; calculator JS file size reduced

description: Fixed 76 non-descriptive anchor text links: CookieConsent 'Learn more' → 'Read our Privacy Policy' (affects every page); homepage banner 'Learn more' → 'Get 3 months free'
reason: Semrush notice: 76 links with non-descriptive anchor text 'Learn more' pointing to /privacy-policy/
expectedImpact: Anchor text quality score improved; better accessibility and crawl context for privacy policy link

description: Fixed hub/pillar links in first 200 words of 6 blog posts (driver-schedule-guide, legal-responsibilities-driving, observation-driving-test, reduce-no-shows-driving-school, schedule-to-drive-guide, student-progress-tracking); added ## Related Articles sections to 14 business posts (all posts lacking a structured related section); converted 3 posts with bold **Related Articles:** to proper ## heading format
reason: Structural audit found 36 posts missing hub links (actually 6 once DVSA pillar was included) and 14 business posts with no Related Articles section — same pattern that was fixed on petcare.software in March 2026. Hub links missing from first 200 words breaks bidirectional cluster authority and reduces crawl equity flowing to pillar pages.
expectedImpact: Improved internal link equity to all 3 pillar pages; better topical authority signals across software and DVSA skills clusters; verify-seo.js inbound link count improvements; should help move 61 non-indexed pages toward indexed status

description: Added AuthorByline component with John Powell photo, bio, and LinkedIn to all 47 blog post templates; enhanced JSON-LD author schema with jobTitle, image URL, and sameAs LinkedIn
reason: Build E-E-A-T signals by attributing all content to a named, credentialed author with LinkedIn verification
expectedImpact: Improved author authority signals in Google Search; richer Person schema may trigger author panels or knowledge graph associations

description: Added 28 contextual internal links across 14 blog posts: /pricing/ linked from 4 posts; /driving-skills/driving-conditions/ from 3 posts; /driving-skills/basics/ from 4 posts; /blog/student-progress-tracking/ from 8 posts; /driving-skills/following-routes/ and /driving-skills/observation-signalling/ each from 1 post
reason: Improve internal link equity distribution, reduce orphan risk for product and skills pages, and increase crawl depth across the blog cluster
expectedImpact: Better PageRank flow to /pricing/ and /driving-skills/ sub-pages; improved indexation of previously underlinked pages

description: Added /changelog/ page with 10 product update entries and footer link in Resources column
reason: New page adds crawlable content, internal link from footer sitewide, and product credibility signal for E-E-A-T
expectedImpact: New page indexed; sitewide footer link passes equity to /changelog/; may improve trust signals

description: Added lastmod timestamps to all sitemap entries; fixed duplicate og:title brand suffix on 11 blog posts with short titles
reason: Sitemap lastmod improves crawl prioritisation; duplicate titles hurt click-through in SERP snippets and confuse scrapers
expectedImpact: Better crawl efficiency; cleaner og:title in social shares and link previews for affected blog posts

description: Published founder story blog post: what-i-learned-building-pet-care-software
reason: E-E-A-T signal — founder's background with 1,000+ customers builds authority; targets informational queries from ADIs researching the product
expectedImpact: Improved E-E-A-T signals; new entry point for brand-aware searches; internal links boost scheduling/management/DVSA pages

description: Added inbound link to /blog/what-i-learned-building-pet-care-software/ from about page (RevelationPets section) to satisfy ≥2 inbound links SEO requirement
reason: New founder blog post had only 1 inbound internal link; verify-seo.js blocked build until fixed
expectedImpact: Post now eligible for indexing; internal link from about page adds topical authority signal

description: Submitted 19 URLs to Google Indexing API (urlNotifications:publish) and resubmitted sitemap: 3 blog posts, /get-started/, /tools/cost-calculator/ + 10 city pages, /, /pricing/, /driving-school-software/, /blog/what-i-learned-building-pet-care-software/
reason: Accelerate indexing of newly published and updated pages — natural crawling takes 2-6 weeks; Indexing API typically indexes within hours
expectedImpact: 19 URLs indexed within 1-3 days instead of weeks
